generatedSentence: An aging philanthropist, professor Elara Vance, known for her knowhow in global ecosystem preservation, felt an inherent worry as the jumbotron in the city square flashed warnings of an approaching meteorite. Her love for the people she sought to nurture was evident, a deep affection unlike the hatred often seen in the marketplace. She knew her jobs were far from over, despite the immemorial lore suggesting an inevitable end. Her diploma in astrophysics wasn't just a piece of paper; it was a commitment to prevent oblivion. The calm city now pulsed with a nascent turmoil, a spark of panic in every mouth. She wasn't one to fool around when the planet's orbit was at risk. A grizzled outlander, a seasoned runner named Kael, arrived at her remote orchard laboratory, his grimy pants and weathered facecloth suggesting a long journey, evading the oppressive regime that sought to reject anyone capable of independent thought. He carried a strange object, an aluminium casing he'd salvaged from a crashed aerodrome, hoping it held the form of a forgotten energy source, perhaps a tasty potato substitute. Professor Vance, though finicky about security, couldn't not be intrigued. He explained that a necromancer-like villain was following them, aiming to crushed their efforts to create a habitable haven from the impending doom, a gator tattoo peeking from his sleeve. Their mission, a desperate racing against time, was tackled head-on as the electric surge from the borrowed lamp illuminating their map caused a nearby kettledrum to resonate. The picky henchmen, led by a ruthless jockey, burst into the lab, their attack creating a cacophony of jams. They sought a mystical jewel rumored to control the city's dormant volcano. The Professor, with an offhand gesture, knocked over a priceless vase of nectar she'd acquired from a distant gallery, sending noodles scattering down the brook. Kael, with his indulgent but effective fighting style, embraces the chaos, creating a risque diversion, while a centipede crawled unnoticed from a mushy rabbeting joint in the fireplace. He knew he had to act sooner. The immediate threat was quelled; nobody was killed, nor murdered, a minor miracle given the chaos. Kael, acting as a makeshift coach, offered the Professor a much-needed vitamin and even some deodorant from her abandoned purse after the intense struggle. She found solace by the kitchen sink, noticing a lone freckle on her hand, a reminder of her humanity. The stolen rom chip was recovered, its data tested and secured, containing vital coordinates to the hidden emeralds. As the halftime of their epic struggle ended, a small ocelot perched calmly on the mantle, symbolizing the quiet hope that was still available to them, a hope akin to Christ's enduring message. The ignorant journalist, an adult suffering from a digestive ailment, was kindly led away, his jackal-like persistence silenced by the rondo of their victory through the terraria gardens. Otherwise, peace had settled, for now.
panelSentences: An aging philanthropist, professor Elara Vance, known for her knowhow in global ecosystem preservation, felt an inherent worry as the jumbotron in the city square flashed warnings of an approaching meteorite. Her love for the people she sought to nurture was evident, a deep affection unlike the hatred often seen in the marketplace. She knew her jobs were far from over, despite the immemorial lore suggesting an inevitable end. Her diploma in astrophysics wasn't just a piece of paper; it was a commitment to prevent oblivion. The calm city now pulsed with a nascent turmoil, a spark of panic in every mouth. She wasn't one to fool around when the planet's orbit was at risk.

A grizzled outlander, a seasoned runner named Kael, arrived at her remote orchard laboratory, his grimy pants and weathered facecloth suggesting a long journey, evading the oppressive regime that sought to reject anyone capable of independent thought. He carried a strange object, an aluminium casing he'd salvaged from a crashed aerodrome, hoping it held the form of a forgotten energy source, perhaps a tasty potato substitute. Professor Vance, though finicky about security, couldn't not be intrigued. He explained that a necromancer-like villain was following them, aiming to crushed their efforts to create a habitable haven from the impending doom, a gator tattoo peeking from his sleeve.

Their mission, a desperate racing against time, was tackled head-on as the electric surge from the borrowed lamp illuminating their map caused a nearby kettledrum to resonate. The picky henchmen, led by a ruthless jockey, burst into the lab, their attack creating a cacophony of jams. They sought a mystical jewel rumored to control the city's dormant volcano. The Professor, with an offhand gesture, knocked over a priceless vase of nectar she'd acquired from a distant gallery, sending noodles scattering down the brook. Kael, with his indulgent but effective fighting style, embraces the chaos, creating a risque diversion, while a centipede crawled unnoticed from a mushy rabbeting joint in the fireplace. He knew he had to act sooner.

The immediate threat was quelled; nobody was killed, nor murdered, a minor miracle given the chaos. Kael, acting as a makeshift coach, offered the Professor a much-needed vitamin and even some deodorant from her abandoned purse after the intense struggle. She found solace by the kitchen sink, noticing a lone freckle on her hand, a reminder of her humanity. The stolen rom chip was recovered, its data tested and secured, containing vital coordinates to the hidden emeralds. As the halftime of their epic struggle ended, a small ocelot perched calmly on the mantle, symbolizing the quiet hope that was still available to them, a hope akin to Christ's enduring message. The ignorant journalist, an adult suffering from a digestive ailment, was kindly led away, his jackal-like persistence silenced by the rondo of their victory through the terraria gardens. Otherwise, peace had settled, for now.
rawPanelSentences: An aging **philanthropist**, **professor** Elara Vance, known for her **knowhow** in **global** **ecosystem** preservation, felt an **inherent** **worry** as the **jumbotron** in the city square flashed warnings of an approaching **meteorite**. Her **love** for the **people** she sought to **nurture** was evident, a deep affection unlike the **hatred** often seen in the **marketplace**. She knew her **jobs** were far from over, despite the **immemorial** **lore** suggesting an inevitable **end**. Her **diploma** in astrophysics wasn't just a piece of paper; it was a commitment to prevent **oblivion**. The calm city now pulsed with a nascent **turmoil**, a **spark** of panic in every **mouth**. She wasn't one to **fool** around when the planet's **orbit** was at risk.

A grizzled **outlander**, a seasoned **runner** named Kael, arrived at her remote **orchard** laboratory, his **grimy** **pants** and weathered **facecloth** suggesting a long journey, **evading** the oppressive **regime** that sought to **reject** anyone capable of independent thought. He carried a strange **object**, an **aluminium** casing he'd **salvage**d from a crashed **aerodrome**, hoping it held the **form** of a forgotten energy source, perhaps a **tasty** **potato** substitute. Professor Vance, though **finicky** about security, couldn't **not** be intrigued. He explained that a **necromancer**-like **villain** was **following** them, aiming to **crushed** their efforts to create a **habitable** **haven** from the impending doom, a **gator** tattoo peeking from his sleeve.

Their mission, a desperate **racing** against time, was **tackled** head-on as the **electric** surge from the **borrowed** **lamp** illuminating their map caused a nearby **kettledrum** to resonate. The **picky** henchmen, led by a ruthless **jockey**, burst into the lab, their attack creating a cacophony of **jams**. They sought a mystical **jewel** rumored to control the city's dormant **volcano**. The Professor, with an **offhand** gesture, knocked over a priceless **vase** of **nectar** she'd acquired from a distant **gallery**, sending **noodles** scattering down the **brook**. Kael, with his **indulgent** but effective fighting style, **embraces** the chaos, creating a **risque** diversion, while a **centipede** crawled unnoticed from a **mushy** **rabbeting** joint in the **fireplace**. He knew he had to act **sooner**.

The immediate threat was quelled; **nobody** was **killed**, nor **murdered**, a minor miracle given the chaos. Kael, acting as a makeshift **coach**, offered the Professor a much-needed **vitamin** and even some **deodorant** from her abandoned **purse** after the intense struggle. She found solace by the kitchen **sink**, noticing a lone **freckle** on her hand, a reminder of her humanity. The stolen **rom** chip was recovered, its data **tested** and secured, containing vital coordinates to the hidden **emeralds**. As the **halftime** of their epic struggle ended, a small **ocelot** perched calmly on the **mantle**, symbolizing the quiet hope that was still **available** to them, a hope akin to **Christ**'s enduring message. The **ignorant** **journalist**, an adult suffering from a **digestive** ailment, was **kindly** led away, his **jackal**-like persistence silenced by the **rondo** of their victory through the **terraria** gardens. **Otherwise**, peace had settled, for now.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neo-noir comic art, thick white gutters, clean black borders, cel-shading, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting. A mature woman, Professor Elara Vance, with discerning eyes, a sharp jawline, and prominent gray streaks in her long, pulled-back hair, wearing a lab coat over practical cargo pants, stands alongside Kael, a grizzled man with a thick, salt-and-pepper beard, visible facial scars, and a sleeveless utility vest, both clearly adult. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Professor Vance intently studying a holographic map of a looming meteorite, a jumbotron visible outside a window, wide shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Kael with his back to the camera, entering a cluttered orchard laboratory, a grimy facecloth hanging from his pocket, over-the-shoulder shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Professor Vance and Kael in a frantic struggle with two adult henchmen in a lab filled with shattered equipment and sparking wires, dynamic mid-shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Professor Vance sitting exhausted by a sink, a hand to her forehead showing a single freckle, Kael offers her a bottle, close-up shot.

definitions.definition: A metallic or stony object or body that is the remains of a meteoroid.
An open area in a town housing a public market.
The space, actual or metaphorical, in which a market operates.
definitions.example: Some high-street retailers were slow to enter the new digital marketplace of the Internet.

definitions.definition: (by extension) The world of commerce and trade.
A place or sphere for the exchange of anything, such as ideas or fashions.
A circular or elliptical path of one object around another object, particularly in astronomy and space travel.
definitions.example: The Moon's orbit around the Earth takes nearly one month to complete.

definitions.definition: A sphere of influence; an area of control.
definitions.example: In the post WWII era, several eastern European countries came into the orbit of the Soviet Union.

definitions.definition: The course of one's usual progression, or the extent of one's typical range.
definitions.example: The convenience store was a heavily travelled point in her daily orbit, as she purchased both cigarettes and lottery tickets there.

definitions.definition: The bony cavity containing the eyeball; the eye socket.
A mathematical function that describes the wave-like behavior of an electron in an atom; area of the highest probability of electron´s occurrence around the atom's nucleus.
A collection of points related by the evolution function of a dynamical system.
The subset of elements of a set X to which a given element can be moved by members of a specified group of transformations that act on X.
A state of increased excitement, activity, or anger.
definitions.example: Dad went into orbit when I told him that I'd crashed the car.

definitions.definition: A path for the ball on the outer edge of the playfield, usually connected so that the ball entering in one end will come out of the other.
To circle or revolve around another object.
definitions.example: The Earth orbits the Sun.

definitions.definition: To move around the general vicinity of something.
definitions.example: The harried mother had a cloud of children orbiting her, begging for sweets.

definitions.definition: To place an object into an orbit around a planet.
definitions.example: A rocket was used to orbit the satellite.

definitions.definition: Other than supposed; different.
definitions.example: He said he didn’t do it, but the evidence was otherwise.

definitions.definition: (manner) Differently, in another way.
definitions.example: Could I do otherwise than smile?.

definitions.definition: In different circumstances; or else.
definitions.example: I’m not well today, otherwise I would have helped.  You have to open your umbrella, otherwise you'll get wet.

definitions.definition: In all other respects.
definitions.example: He lost his temper once in a while. Otherwise he behaved rationally.

definitions.definition: The knowledge and skill to be able to (do something correctly).
definitions.example: Marlo has the know-how to fix your computer.

definitions.definition: A precious or semi-precious stone; gem, gemstone.
A valuable object used for personal ornamentation, especially one made of precious metals and stones; a piece of jewellery.
Anything precious or valuable.
definitions.example: Galveston was the jewel of Texas prior to the hurricane.

definitions.definition: A bearing for a pivot in a watch, formed of a crystal or precious stone.
Any of various lycaenid butterflies of the genus Hypochrysops.
The clitoris.
To bejewel; to decorate or bedeck with jewels or gems.
A very large video screen, typically used in a stadium or concert venue to show close-up shots.

A vent or fissure on the surface of a planet (usually in a mountainous form) with a magma chamber attached to the mantle of a planet or moon, periodically erupting forth lava and volcanic gases onto the surface.
A form of firework.
Any arthropod of class Chilopoda, which have a segmented body with one pair of legs per segment and from about 20 to 300 legs in total.
A figure or other artistic depiction of Jesus Christ.
An enclosure wherein very small animals are displayed humanely, often with some plants, in a naturalistic setting.
A partially enclosed glass container for displaying plants, especially plants that need high humidity.
To force a person to the ground with the weight of one's own body, usually by jumping on top or slamming one's weight into him or her.
To face or deal with, attempting to overcome or fight down.
definitions.example: The government's measures to tackle crime were insufficient.

definitions.definition: To attempt to take away a ball.
To bring a ball carrier to the ground.
To "hit on" or pursue a person that one is interested in.
Made of ropes tackled together.
A person with poor judgment or little intelligence.
definitions.example: The village fool threw his own shoes down the well.

definitions.definition: A jester; a person whose role was to entertain a sovereign and the court (or lower personages).
Someone who derives pleasure from something specified.
Buddy, dude, man.
A type of dessert made of puréed fruit and custard or cream.
definitions.example: an apricot fool; a gooseberry fool

definitions.definition: (often capitalized, Fool) A particular card in a tarot deck, representing a jester.
To trick; to deceive
To act in an idiotic manner; to act foolishly
Foolish.
To go after; to pursue; to move behind in the same path or direction.
definitions.example: Follow that car!

definitions.definition: To go or come after in a sequence.
definitions.example: B follows A in the alphabet.

definitions.definition: To carry out (orders, instructions, etc.).
definitions.example: Follow these instructions to the letter.

definitions.definition: To live one's life according to (religion, teachings, etc).
To understand, to pay attention to.
definitions.example: Do you follow me?

definitions.definition: To watch, to keep track of (reports of) some event or person.
definitions.example: I followed the incumbent throughout the election.

definitions.definition: To subscribe to see content from an account on a social media platform.
definitions.example: If you want to see more of our articles, follow us on Twitter.

definitions.definition: To be a logical consequence of something.
definitions.example: If you don't practise proper hygiene, illness is sure to follow.

definitions.definition: To walk in, as a road or course; to attend upon closely, as a profession or calling.
A group of followers, attendants or admirers; an entourage.
definitions.example: He had a loyal following.

definitions.definition: Vocation; business; profession.
(with definite article, treated as singular or plural) A thing or things to be mentioned immediately after.
definitions.example: The following are the three most important questions.

definitions.definition: Coming next, either in sequence or in time.
definitions.example: See the following section.

definitions.definition: About to be specified.
definitions.example: The following words have no definition [...]

definitions.definition: (of a wind) Blowing in the direction of travel.
definitions.example: The following wind sped us on our way.

definitions.definition: After, subsequent to.
definitions.example: Following the meeting, we all had a chat.

definitions.definition: The tuber of a plant, Solanum tuberosum, eaten as a starchy vegetable, particularly in the Americas and Europe; this plant.
A conspicuous hole in a sock or stocking
A camera that takes poor-quality pictures.
A mentally handicapped person.
An underpowered computer or other device, especially when small in size.
A person who loves humankind in general.
A very generous person or institution.
Strong aversion; intense dislike
All the facts and traditions about a particular subject that have been accumulated over time through education or experience.
definitions.example: the lore of the Ancient Egyptians

definitions.definition: The backstory created around a fictional universe.
Workmanship.
Strong affection.
A person who is the object of romantic feelings; a darling, a sweetheart, a beloved.
A term of friendly address, regardless of feelings.
definitions.example: Hello love, how can I help you?

definitions.definition: A thing, activity etc which is the object of one's deep liking or enthusiasm.
Sexual desire; attachment based on sexual attraction.
Sexual activity.
An instance or episode of being in love; a love affair.
Used as the closing, before the signature, of a letter, especially between good friends or family members, or by the young.
Alternative letter-case form of Love.
A thin silk material.
A climbing plant, Clematis vitalba.
(usually transitive, sometimes intransitive, stative) To have a strong affection for (someone or something).
definitions.example: I love my spouse.   I love you!

definitions.definition: To need, thrive on.
definitions.example: Mold loves moist, dark places.

definitions.definition: To be strongly inclined towards something; an emphatic form of like.
definitions.example: I love walking barefoot on wet grass;  I'd love to join the team;  I love what you've done with your hair

definitions.definition: (usually transitive, sometimes intransitive) To care deeply about, to be dedicated to (someone or something).
To derive delight from a fact or situation.
definitions.example: I love the fact that the coffee shop now offers fat-free chai latte.

definitions.definition: To have sex with (perhaps from make love).
definitions.example: I wish I could love her all night long.

definitions.definition: A foreigner or alien.
A stranger or outsider.
To put to death; to extinguish the life of.
definitions.example: Smoking kills more people each year than alcohol and drugs combined.

definitions.definition: To render inoperative.
definitions.example: He killed the engine and turned off the headlights, but remained in the car, waiting.

definitions.definition: To stop, cease or render void; to terminate.
definitions.example: My computer wouldn't respond until I killed some of the running processes.

definitions.definition: To amaze, exceed, stun or otherwise incapacitate.
definitions.example: That joke always kills me.

definitions.definition: To cause great pain, discomfort or distress to.
definitions.example: These tight shoes are killing my feet.

definitions.definition: To produce feelings of dissatisfaction or revulsion in.
definitions.example: It kills me to learn how many poor people are practically starving in this country while rich moguls spend such outrageous amounts on useless luxuries.

definitions.definition: To use up or to waste.
definitions.example: He told the bartender, pointing at the bottle of scotch he planned to consume, "Leave it, I'm going to kill the bottle."

definitions.definition: To exert an overwhelming effect on.
definitions.example: Between the two of us, we killed the rest of the case of beer.

definitions.definition: To overpower, overwhelm or defeat.
definitions.example: The team had absolutely killed their traditional rivals, and the local sports bars were raucous with celebrations.

definitions.definition: To force a company out of business.
To produce intense pain.
definitions.example: You don't ever want to get rabies. The doctor will have to give you multiple shots and they really kill.

definitions.definition: To punish severely.
definitions.example: My parents are going to kill me!

definitions.definition: To strike (a ball, etc.) with such force and placement as to make a shot that is impossible to defend against, usually winning a point.
To cause (a ball, etc.) to be out of play, resulting in a stoppage of gameplay.
To succeed with an audience, especially in comedy.
To cause to assume the value zero.
(IRC) To disconnect (a user) involuntarily from the network.
To deadmelt.
(of steel) deoxidized
To take part in a race (in the sense of a contest).
definitions.example: The drivers were racing around the track.

definitions.definition: To compete against in such a race.
definitions.example: I raced him to the car, but he was there first, so he got to ride shotgun.

definitions.definition: To move or drive at high speed; to hurry or speed.
definitions.example: As soon as it was time to go home, he raced for the door.

definitions.definition: Of a motor, to run rapidly when not engaged to a transmission.
To assign a race to; to perceive as having a (usually specified) race.
The sport of competing in races.
To demolish; to level to the ground.
To scrape as if with a razor.
A document issued by an educational institution testifying that the recipient has earned a degree or has successfully completed a particular course of study.
A substance that aids digestion.
A digestive biscuit.
definitions.example: I bought a packet of chocolate digestives.

definitions.definition: Of, relating to, or functioning in digestion.
That causes or promotes digestion.
Such as one may avail oneself of; capable of being used for the accomplishment of a purpose.
definitions.example: We have an available candidate.

definitions.definition: Readily obtainable.
definitions.example: The list shows the available products in the store.

definitions.definition: Valid.
definitions.example: This is an available plea.

definitions.definition: Having sufficient power, force, or efficacy to achieve the purpose; availing, effective.
Free to meet someone, speak on the telephone, enter a romantic relationship or the like.
definitions.example: Hi, this is Mark Smith calling. Is your mother available?

definitions.definition: Naturally as part or consequence of something.
Short in length of time from the present.
definitions.example: I need the soonest date you have available.

definitions.definition: Early
Immediately, instantly.
Within a short time; quickly.
Early.
Readily; willingly; used with would, or some other word expressing will.

Any of a specific group of organic compounds essential in small quantities for healthy human growth, metabolism, development, and body function; found in minute amounts in plant and animal foods or sometimes produced synthetically; deficiencies of specific vitamins produce specific disorders.
Alligator.
(heading, physical) To do with shape.
(social) To do with structure or procedure.
A blank document or template to be filled in by the user.
definitions.example: To apply for the position, complete the application form.

definitions.definition: A specimen document to be copied or imitated.
Level of performance.
definitions.example: The orchestra was on top form this evening.

definitions.definition: (grammar) A grouping of words which maintain grammatical context in different usages; the particular shape or structure of a word or part of speech.
definitions.example: participial forms;  verb forms

definitions.definition: The den or home of a hare.
A window or dialogue box.
An infraspecific rank.
The type or other matter from which an impression is to be taken, arranged and secured in a chase.
A quantic.
(fitness) A specific way of performing a movement.
To assume (a certain shape or visible structure).
definitions.example: When you kids form a straight line I'll hand out the lollies.

definitions.definition: To give (a shape or visible structure) to a thing or person.
definitions.example: Roll out the dough to form a thin sheet.

definitions.definition: To take shape.
definitions.example: When icicles start to form on the eaves you know the roads will be icy.

definitions.definition: To put together or bring into being; assemble.
definitions.example: Paul McCartney and John Lennon formed The Beatles in Liverpool in 1960.

definitions.definition: To create (a word) by inflection or derivation.
definitions.example: By adding "-ness", you can form a noun from an adjective.

definitions.definition: To constitute, to compose, to make up.
definitions.example: Teenagers form the bulk of extreme traffic offenders.

definitions.definition: To mould or model by instruction or discipline.
definitions.example: Singing in a choir helps to form a child's sociality.

definitions.definition: To provide (a hare) with a form.

To treat (plates) to prepare them for introduction into a storage battery, causing one plate to be composed more or less of spongy lead, and the other of lead peroxide. This was formerly done by repeated slow alternations of the charging current, but later the plates or grids were coated or filled, one with a paste of red lead and the other with litharge, introduced into the cell, and formed by a direct charging current.
A small brownish or reddish pigmentation spot on the surface of the skin.
definitions.example: Steve has brown hair, blue eyes, and freckles on his cheeks and nose.

definitions.definition: Any small spot or discoloration.
A small sweet consisting of a flattish mound of chocolate covered in hundreds and thousands.
The anus.
To cover with freckles.
To become covered with freckles.
Used as plural of person; a body of human beings considered generally or collectively; a group of two or more persons.
definitions.example: There were so many people at the restaurant last night.

definitions.definition: Persons forming or belonging to a particular group, such as a nation, class, ethnic group, country, family, etc.
A group of persons regarded as being employees, followers, companions or subjects of a ruler.
One's colleagues or employees.
A person's ancestors, relatives or family.
definitions.example: My people lived through the Black Plague and the Thirty Years War.

definitions.definition: The mass of a community as distinguished from a special class (elite); the commonalty; the populace; the vulgar; the common crowd; the citizens.
To stock with people or inhabitants; to fill as with people; to populate.
To become populous or populated.
To inhabit; to occupy; to populate.
To interact with people; to socialize.
An individual; usually a human being.
definitions.example: Each person is unique, both mentally and physically.

definitions.definition: The physical body of a being seen as distinct from the mind, character, etc.
Any individual or formal organization with standing before the courts.
definitions.example: At common law a corporation or a trust is legally a person.

definitions.definition: The human genitalia; specifically, the penis.
(grammar) A linguistic category used to distinguish between the speaker of an utterance and those to whom or about whom he or she is speaking. See grammatical person.
A shoot or bud of a plant; a polyp or zooid of the compound Hydrozoa, Anthozoa, etc.; also, an individual, in the narrowest sense, among the higher animals.
(usually with definite article) Electricity; the electricity supply.
definitions.example: We had to sit in the dark because the electric was cut off.

definitions.definition: An electric car.
An electric toothbrush.
A substance or object which can be electrified; an insulator or non-conductor, like amber or glass.
Fencing with the use of a body wire, box, and related equipment to detect when a weapon has touched an opponent.
Of, relating to, produced by, operated with, or utilising electricity; electrical.
Of or relating to an electronic version of a musical instrument that has an acoustic equivalent.
Being emotionally thrilling; electrifying.
To receive (something) from somebody temporarily, expecting to return it.
To take money from a bank under the agreement that the bank will be paid over the course of time.
To adopt (an idea) as one's own.
definitions.example: to borrow the style, manner, or opinions of another

definitions.definition: To adopt a word from another language.

In a subtraction, to deduct (one) from a digit of the minuend and add ten to the following digit, in order that the subtraction of a larger digit in the subtrahend from the digit in the minuend to which ten is added gives a positive result.
(Upper Midwestern United States) To lend.
To temporarily obtain (something) for (someone).
To feign or counterfeit.
(obsolete except in ballads) To secure the release of (someone) from prison.
To receive (something) from somebody, with little possibility of returning it.
definitions.example: Can I borrow a sheet of paper?

definitions.definition: A device that generates heat, light or other radiation. Especially an electric light bulb.
A device containing oil, burnt through a wick for illumination; an oil lamp.
A piece of furniture holding one or more electric light sockets.
To hit, clout, belt, wallop.
To hunt at night using a lamp; see lamping.
To hang out or chill; to do nothing in particular.
A strong feeling of anxiety.
definitions.example: I'm afflicted by worry throughout the night.

definitions.definition: An instance or cause of such a feeling.
definitions.example: My main worry is that I'll miss the train.

definitions.definition: To be troubled; to give way to mental anxiety or doubt.
definitions.example: Stop worrying about your test, it’ll be fine.

definitions.definition: Disturb the peace of mind of; afflict with mental agitation or distress.
definitions.example: Your tone of voice worries me.

definitions.definition: To harass; to irritate or distress.
definitions.example: The President was worried into military action by persistent advisors.

definitions.definition: To seize or shake by the throat, especially of a dog or wolf.
definitions.example: Your dog’s been worrying sheep again.

definitions.definition: To touch repeatedly, to fiddle with.
To strangle.
The opening of a creature through which food is ingested.
definitions.example: "Open your mouth and say 'aah'," directed the doctor.

definitions.definition: The end of a river out of which water flows into a sea or other large body of water.
definitions.example: The mouth of the river is a good place to go birdwatching in spring and autumn.

definitions.definition: An outlet, aperture or orifice.
definitions.example: The mouth of a cave

definitions.definition: A loud or overly talkative person.
definitions.example: My kid sister is a real mouth; she never shuts up.

definitions.definition: (saddlery) The crosspiece of a bridle bit, which enters the mouth of an animal.
A principal speaker; one who utters the common opinion; a mouthpiece.
Cry; voice.
Speech; language; testimony.
A wry face; a grimace; a mow.
To speak; to utter.

definitions.definition: To make the actions of speech, without producing sound.
definitions.example: The prompter mouthed the words to the actor, who had forgotten them.

definitions.definition: To utter with a voice that is overly loud or swelling.
To pick up or handle with the lips or mouth, but not chew or swallow.
definitions.example: The fish mouthed the lure, but didn't bite.

definitions.definition: To take into the mouth; to seize or grind with the mouth or teeth; to chew; to devour.
To form or cleanse with the mouth; to lick, as a bear licks her cub.
To make mouths at.
One who rides racehorses competitively.
That part of a variable resistor or potentiometer that rides over the resistance wire
An operator of some machinery or apparatus.
A dealer in horses; a horse trader.
A cheat; one given to sharp practice in trade.
A prostitute's client.
A rapist.
To ride (a horse) in a race.
To jostle by riding against.
definitions.example: They were jockeying for position toward the end of the race.

definitions.definition: To maneuver (something) by skill for one's advantage.
To cheat or trick.
A sweet mixture of fruit boiled with sugar and allowed to congeal. Often spread on bread or toast or used in jam tarts.
A difficult situation.
Blockage, congestion.
definitions.example: A traffic jam caused us to miss the game's first period.

definitions.definition: An informal, impromptu performance or rehearsal.
(by extension) A song; a track.
(by extension) An informal event where people brainstorm and collaborate on projects.
To get something stuck in a confined space.

definitions.definition: To brusquely force something into a space; cram, squeeze.
definitions.example: The rush-hour train was jammed with commuters.

definitions.definition: To cause congestion or blockage. Often used with "up"
definitions.example: A single accident can jam the roads for hours.

definitions.definition: To block or confuse a broadcast signal.
To throw a pitch at or near the batter's hands.
definitions.example: Jones was jammed by the pitch.

definitions.definition: To play music (especially improvisation as a group, or an informal unrehearsed session).
To injure a finger or toe by sudden compression of the digit's tip.
definitions.example: When he tripped on the step he jammed his toe.

definitions.definition: To attempt to score points.
definitions.example: Toughie jammed four times in the second period.

definitions.definition: To bring (a vessel) so close to the wind that half her upper sails are laid aback.
To give up on a date or some joint endeavour; stand up, chicken out, jam out.
A kind of frock for children.
(interior decorating) Either of the vertical components that form the side of an opening in a wall, such as that of a door frame, window frame, or fireplace.
Any thick mass of rock that prevents miners from following the lode or vein.
Any of certain wild canids of the genus Canis, native to the tropical Old World and smaller than a wolf.
A person who performs menial/routine tasks, a dogsbody.
A person who behaves in an opportunistic way; especially a base collaborator.
A jack (the playing card).
To perform menial or routine tasks
A task.
definitions.example: A job half done is hardly done at all.

definitions.definition: An economic role for which a person is paid.
definitions.example: He's been out of a job since being made redundant in January.

definitions.definition: (in noun compounds) Plastic surgery.
definitions.example: He had had a nose job.

definitions.definition: A task, or series of tasks, carried out in batch mode (especially on a mainframe computer).
A sudden thrust or stab; a jab.
A public transaction done for private profit; something performed ostensibly as a part of official duty, but really for private gain; a corrupt official business.
Any affair or event which affects one, whether fortunately or unfortunately.
A thing (often used in a vague way to refer to something whose name one cannot recall).

definitions.definition: To do odd jobs or occasional work for hire.
To work as a jobber.
To take the loss.
To buy and sell for profit, as securities; to speculate in.

definitions.definition: To seek private gain under pretence of public service; to turn public matters to private advantage.
To strike or stab with a pointed instrument.
To thrust in, as a pointed instrument.
To hire or let in periods of service.

definitions.definition: Act or instance of running, of moving rapidly using the feet.

definitions.definition: Act or instance of hurrying (to or from a place) (not necessarily by foot); dash or errand, trip.

definitions.definition: A pleasure trip.

definitions.definition: Flight, instance or period of fleeing.
Migration (of fish).
A group of fish that migrate, or ascend a river for the purpose of spawning.
A path taken by literal movement or figuratively
An enclosure for an animal; a track or path along which something can travel.

definitions.definition: Rural landholding for farming, usually for running sheep, and operated by a runholder.
State of being current; currency; popularity.
Continuous or sequential
A flow of liquid; a leak.

definitions.definition: (West Virginia) A small creek or part thereof. (Compare Southern US branch and New York and New England brook.)

definitions.definition: A quick pace, faster than a walk.

definitions.definition: A sudden series of demands on a bank or other financial institution, especially characterised by great withdrawals.

definitions.definition: Any sudden large demand for something.

definitions.definition: Various horizontal dimensions or surfaces
A standard or unexceptional group or category.

definitions.definition: In sports
A line of knit stitches that have unravelled, particularly in a nylon stocking.
definitions.example: I have a run in my stocking.

definitions.definition: The stern of the underwater body of a ship from where it begins to curve upward and inward.

The horizontal distance to which a drift may be carried, either by licence of the proprietor of a mine or by the nature of the formation; also, the direction which a vein of ore or other substance takes.
A pair or set of millstones.
(usually in the phrase 'do a runner') A quick escape away from a scene.

definitions.definition: A deserter.
A type of soft-soled shoe originally intended for runners.
Part of a shoe that is stitched to the bottom of the upper so it can be glued to the sole.
A part of an apparatus that moves quickly.

definitions.definition: A mechanical part intended to guide or aid something else to move (using wheels or sliding).
An automobile; a working or driveable automobile.

definitions.definition: A strip of fabric used to decorate or protect a table or dressing table.

definitions.definition: A long, narrow carpet for a high traffic area such as a hall or stairs.

definitions.definition: A part of a cigarette that is burning unevenly.
A long stolon sent out by a plant (such as strawberry), in order to root new plantlets, or a plant that propagates by using such runners.
A short sling with a carabiner on either end, used to link the climbing rope to a bolt or other protection such as a nut or friend.
A competitor in a poker tournament.
A restaurant employee responsible for taking food from the kitchens to the tables.
A leaping food fish (Elagatis pinnulatis) of Florida and the West Indies; the skipjack, shoemaker, or yellowtail.
(sports slang) An employee of a sports agent who tries to recruit possible player clients for the agent.
A rope to increase the power of a tackle.
A speedrunner.
An idea or plan that has potential to be adopted or put into operation.

definitions.definition: That is beyond memory; ancient.

definitions.definition: (positive) Ancient beyond memory.
definitions.example: The immemorial existence of a port on the spot would shew such a possible good legal origin as would support the immemorial custom.

definitions.definition: A vile, wicked person.
In fiction, a character who has the role of being bad, especially antagonizing the hero.
Any opponent player, especially a hypothetical player for example and didactic purposes. Compare: hero.

definitions.definition: To debase; to degrade.
A feudal tenant.
An upright open container used mainly for displaying fresh, dried, or artificial flowers.
The body of the Corinthian capital.
A unary operation on logical values that changes true to false, and false to true.
Negates the meaning of the modified verb.

definitions.definition: To no degree.

definitions.definition: (understatement, litotes) Used to modify superlatives to indicate the opposite or near opposite, often in a form of understatement.

definitions.definition: And not.

definitions.definition: (1990s) Used to indicate that the previous phrase was meant sarcastically or ironically.

definitions.definition: Having a pleasant or satisfying flavor; delicious.

definitions.definition: Having or showing good taste; tasteful.

definitions.definition: Appealing; when applied to persons, sexually appealing.
Skillful; highly competent.
Potentially violent.
A state of great disorder or uncertainty.
Harassing labour; trouble; disturbance.

To harass with commotion; to disquiet; to worry.
Stained or covered with grime.
From the urban musical genre called grime.
A globally scoped identifier.
Concerning all parts of the world.

definitions.definition: Pertaining to the whole of something; total, universal:
Spherical, ball-shaped.

definitions.definition: Of or relating to a globe or sphere.
In the global manner; world-wide.

One who professes something, such as a religious doctrine.
A pianist in a saloon, brothel, etc.

An undergarment that covers the genitals and often the buttocks and the neighbouring parts of the body; underpants.

Full acceptance (of something).
An act of enfolding or including.

To seize (something) eagerly or with alacrity; to accept or take up with cordiality; to welcome.

definitions.definition: To submit to; to undergo.
To encircle; to enclose, to encompass.

definitions.definition: To fasten on, as armour.

To earn; deserve.

definitions.definition: Without planning or thinking ahead.

definitions.definition: Curt, abrupt, unfriendly.

definitions.definition: Right away, immediately, without thinking about it.

definitions.definition: In an offhand manner.
A garden or an area of land for the cultivation of fruit or nut trees.
The trees themselves cultivated in such an area.
The state of forgetting completely, of being oblivious, unconscious, unaware, as when sleeping, drunk, or dead.

definitions.definition: The state of being completely forgotten, of being reduced to a state of non-existence, extinction, or nothingness, incl. through war and destruction. (Figuratively) for an area like hell, a wasteland.

definitions.definition: Amnesty.
To consign to oblivion; to efface utterly.
The keeper of a personal journal, who writes in it regularly.

A single atom of this element.
Aircraft or other machinery made partially or wholly of aluminium.
A small particle of glowing matter, either molten or on fire.
A short or small burst of electrical discharge.
A small, shining body, or transient light; a sparkle.

Any of various lycaenid butterflies of the Indomalayan genus Sinthusa.

An electrician.
To trigger, kindle into activity (an argument, etc).
To light; to kindle.
To give off a spark or sparks.
Appalachian To court.
The drink of the gods.
(by extension) Any delicious drink, now especially a type of sweetened fruit juice.
The sweet liquid secreted by flowers to attract pollinating insects and birds.
To feed on nectar.
(usually in the plural) a string or strip of pasta

The brain, the head

Someone who is not important or well-known.

The uppermost seating area projecting from the rear or side walls of a theater, concert hall, or auditorium.

The spectators of an event, collectively.

A level or drive in a mine.
To show off

To press (one's lips) in and together so that they protrude.

To put into a purse.
To steal purses; to rob.

That which is left; a remnant; a fragment; a scrap.

(in the plural) Money.

definitions.definition: To finish, terminate.

A thing that has physical existence.
Objective; the goal, end or purpose of something.

A person or thing toward which an emotion is directed.

Sight; show; appearance; aspect.

Having a kind personality; kind, warmhearted, sympathetic.

definitions.definition: Favourable, gentle, pleasant, tidy, auspicious, beneficent.
Lawful.
Natural; inherent to the kind or race.
One who is ignorant.
Unknowledgeable or uneducated; characterized by ignorance.
Not knowing (a fact or facts), unaware (of something).
Ill-mannered, crude.

Resulting from ignorance; foolish; silly.

definitions.definition: To refine (gold, silver, etc.) in a test or cupel; to subject to cupellation.

To deliberately kill (a person or persons) without justification, especially with malice aforethought.

To devour, ravish.

definitions.definition: That has been murdered.
The shelf above a fireplace which may be also a structural support for the masonry of the chimney.
A maneuver to surmount a ledge, involving pushing down on the ledge to bring up the body. Also called a mantelshelf.
A piece of clothing somewhat like an open robe or cloak, especially that worn by Orthodox bishops. (Compare mantum.)
A figurative garment representing authority or status, capable of affording protection.

definitions.definition: Anything that covers or conceals something else; a cloak.
The body wall of a mollusc, from which the shell is secreted.
The back of a bird together with the folded wings.
The zone of hot gases around a flame.

A mantling.

To overcome completely; to subdue totally.
definitions.example: The sultan's black guard crushed every resistance bloodily.

definitions.definition: To be or become broken down or in, or pressed into a smaller compass, by external weight or force
definitions.example: an eggshell crushes easily

definitions.definition: To feel infatuation or unrequited love.
definitions.example: She's crushing on him.

definitions.definition: To give a compressed or foreshortened appearance to.
Pulverized, rendered into small, disconnected fragments.
Broken, saddened, depressed.
Of a fabric, having the appearance of having been crushed.
definitions.example: Her top was of black crushed velvet.

definitions.definition: An open hearth for holding a fire at the base of a chimney.
A flannel for washing the face.
A cloth laid over the face of a corpse.
To get away from by cunning; to avoid by dexterity, subterfuge, address, or ingenuity; to elude; to cleverly escape from
definitions.example: He evaded his opponent's blows.

definitions.definition: To escape; to slip away; — sometimes with from.
To attempt to escape; to practice artifice or sophistry, for the purpose of eluding.
A large hemispherical brass percussion instrument (one of the timpani) with a drumhead that can be tuned by adjusting its tension.
An informal social party at which a light collation is offered, held in the afternoon or early evening.
Suggestive of sexual impropriety; bordering on the indelicate.
Any agent acting to eliminate, reduce, mask, or control odor.
An odor-controlling substance applied to the underarm to counteract odor from perspiration.
Acting or including an agent to eliminate, reduce, mask, or control odor
A basin used for holding water for washing.
A drain for carrying off wastewater.
A sinkhole.
A depression in land where water collects, with no visible outlet.
A heat sink.
A place that absorbs resources or energy.
The motion of a sinker pitch.
definitions.example: Jones has a two-seamer with heavy sink.

definitions.definition: An object or callback that captures events; event sink
A destination vertex in a transportation network
An abode of degraded persons; a wretched place.
A depression in a stereotype plate.
A stage trap-door for shifting scenery.
An excavation less than a shaft.
(game development) One or several systems that remove currency from the game's economy, thus controlling or preventing inflation
(heading, physical) To move or be moved into something.
(heading, social) To diminish or be diminished.
To conceal and appropriate.
To keep out of sight; to suppress; to ignore.
To pay absolutely.
definitions.example: I have sunk thousands of pounds into this project.

definitions.definition: To reduce or extinguish by payment.
definitions.example: to sink the national debt

definitions.definition: To be overwhelmed or depressed; to fail in strength.
To decrease in volume, as a river; to subside; to become diminished in volume or in apparent height.
A person who practices or performs necromancy.
A picture.
Fussy; particular; demanding to have things just right.
definitions.example: I am very picky about the way my kitchen is laid out.

definitions.definition: An American feline carnivore (Felis pardalis) covered with blackish ocellated spots and blotches which are variously arranged.
